rulings.Case Title: AVANT CANYON GROUP INC DBA CANYON CONSTRUCTION VS. CAROLYN APIGIAN ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Release Of Mechanics Lien And Lis Pendens, After Dismissal Of Lawsuit For Lack Of Prosecution
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion Calendar for Friday, August 7, 2020, Line 1, DEFENDANTS CAROLYN APIGIAN, MICHAEL APIGIAN, AND CAROLYN APIGIAN AS TRUSTEE OF THE APIGIAN FAMILY'S Motion For Release Of Mechanics Lien And Lis Pendens, After Dismissal Of Lawsuit For Lack Of Prosecution. Defendants Carolyn Apigian, Michael Apigian and Apigian Family Trust's unopposed "motion for release of mechanic's lien and lis pendens, after dismissal of lawsuit for lack of prosecution" is granted. As a result of the COVID-19 emergency, all attorneys and parties are required to appear remotely. Hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. To appear at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the defendants is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/RBU)

rulings.Case Title: THEDA FUNG VS. SUSANA MANCIA ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Plaintiff Theda Fung?S Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Responses To Theda Fung?S Supplemental Interrogatories To Samir F. Daher
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, August 7, 2020, Line 1, 1-PLAINTIFF THEDA FUNG's Motion To Compel Further Responses To Theda Fung?S Supplemental Interrogatories To Samir F. Daher Pro Tem Judge Katherine Gallo, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Parties to appear. As a result of the COVID-19 emergency, all attorneys and parties are required to appear remotely. Hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. To appear at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to Klgallo@discoveryreferee.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If parties have arranged for a court reporter to appear, please include the following in your email: (1) ensure that they are California Certified Shorthand Reporters; (2) the reporter's full contact information [name, CSR number, personal work email, and phone number]. If the reporter is not a California Certified Shorthand Reporter, the transcript cannot be certified for use in California courts. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must e-mail it to the Judge Pro Tem. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: 1830 17TH STREET LLC VS. SENTINEL INSURANCE COMPANY LTD ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion For Summary Judgment Or In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication (Part 2 Of 2 For Purposes Of Entry Of Tentative Ruling)
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion Calendar for Friday, August 7, 2020, Line 7, DEFENDANTS SENTINEL INSURANCE COMPANY LTD AND JEFFREY KAISER'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or, In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication (Part 2 of 2 for Purposes of Entry of Tentative Ruling). Breach of Implied Covenant. Sentinel argues that "plaintiff's bad faith claim is barred by the genuine dispute doctrine." (Memo. 16:13.) However, as Sentinel concedes, "the reasonableness of an insurer's claims-handling conduct is ordinarily a question of fact" and only "becomes a question of law where the evidence is undisputed and only one reasonable inference can be drawn from the evidence." (Id. at 17:2-4; quoting Chateau Chamberay Homeowners Assn. v. Associated Internat. Ins. Co. (2001) 90 Cal.App.4th 335, 346.) Here, as addressed above, the evidence is disputed and more than one reasonable inference can often be drawn. Negligent Misrepresentation. This cause of action is against adjuster Kaiser and not Sentinel. Passing whether Kaiser's statements regarding policy coverages were facts or opinions, plaintiff fails to show other requisite elements of negligent misrepresentation: reasonable reliance and that reliance was a substantial factor in causing harm. (California Civil Jury Instructions 1903(5),(7).) Plaintiff says it "spent significant time and money trying to get Jeffrey Kaiser" to change his coverage opinions that it believed were wrong. (Opp. 17:7-11.) This is the opposite of reliance. Punitive Damages. This part of the motion can be granted only if "no reasonable jury could find the plaintiff's evidence to be clear and convincing proof of malice, fraud or oppression." (Opp. 17:17-19, quoting Hoch v. Allied-Signal, Inc. (1998) 24 Cal.App.4th 48, 58-61; see also Barton v. Alexander Hamilton Life Ins. Co. of America (2003) 110 Cal.App.4th 1640, 1644 (nonsuit test "substantially identical" to summary judgment motion). I cannot make such a finding on the record before me. "Duties to Pay." Sentinel's notice of motion lists "duties to pay" "for repairs to the building" and "lost business income." To the extent such narrow "duties" are susceptible to summary adjudication at all, there is a split of authority as to whether Code of Civil Procedure §437c permits adjudication of less than entire cause of action. (See Edmon & Karnow, California Practice Guide: Civil Procedure Before Trial (TRG 2016) §10:44.2, citing Regan Roofing Co., Inc. v. Sup. Ct. (1994) 24 Cal.App.4th 425, 436.) Even courts on the split's other side give trial courts discretion to rule on a duty issue or not. (Linden Partners v. Wilshire Linden Associates (1998) 62 Cal.App.4th 508, 519.) As a result of the COVID-19 emergency, all attorneys and parties are required to appear remotely. Hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. To appear at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the plaintiff is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. (End of Tentative Ruling) =(302/RBU)

rulings.Case Title: 1830 17TH STREET LLC VS. SENTINEL INSURANCE COMPANY LTD ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion Calendar for Friday, August 7, 2020, Line 7, DEFENDANTS SENTINEL INSURANCE COMPANY LTD AND JEFFREY KAISER'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or, In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication (Part 1 of 2 for Purposes of Entry of Tentative Ruling). Defendant Sentinel Insurance Company, Ltd.'s summary judgment and summary adjudication motions are denied. Defendant Jeffrey Kaiser's summary judgment motion is granted. Breach of Contract. Plaintiff alleges Sentinel breached the insurance contract in five ways: First, Sentinel "applied an overly narrow definition of collapse damage to only include visible damage," when Sentinel knew coverage actually extended to "hidden decay." (Opp. 8:10-28.) Sentinel makes essentially no response to this, arguing portions of a deposition that do not address the policy language. (Rply. 3:15-4:1.) (My order cites to the parties' briefs and the citations therein, rather than their separate statements, because the latter total 217 pages.) True, plaintiff's complaint is general, but Sentinel demonstrates no effort to cabin the issues through discovery. Second, Sentinel did not acknowledge "there is imminent collapse coverage" under its policy. (Opp. 9:1-11:5.) Plaintiff cites policy language providing coverage for "risks of collapse" caused by, e.g., hidden decay. (Id. at 9:6-9.) Sentinel cites other policy language that excludes coverage for "any part of a building that is in danger of falling down." (Rply. 6:5-9.) This indicates an ambiguity as to which further evidence would be needed at trial. Third, Sentinel insurance adjuster Kaiser improperly "withheld policy benefits." (Opp. 11:6-22.) The parties dispute these amounts for a natural gas bill and "lost rent." (Id.; Rply. 5:7-25.) Fourth, Sentinel "failed to investigate the extent of the hidden decay damage." (Opp. 11:28-12:9.) Sentinel disputes by citing an engineer's report of "no signs of deterioration from wood decayalong any portion of the fractured members" (which does not address the unfractured members). (Rply. 4:2-23.) Fifth, Sentinel "failed to pay for the $1.3 million repair." (Opp. 12:10-16.) Sentinel says plaintiff cited an inapposite Insurance Code section. (Rply. 4:24-27.) In sum, Sentinel failed to carry its burdens of production and persuasion on the breach of contract cause of action; triable issues exist. (CCP §437c.) (Tentative Ruling Continues in Part 2 of 2)=(302/RBU)

rulings.Case Title: ADAM FUMAROLA VS. UBER TECHNOLOGIES, INC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant Rasier, Llc, Rasier-Ca, Llc, Uber Technologies, Inc. Defendants Uber Technologies Inc Rasier Llc And Rasier-Ca Llcs Noitice Of Motion And Motion For Relief For Waiver Of Objections (Per Agreement Of The Parties. Email Dated 7/14/20.)
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, August 7, 2020, Line 3, 1-Defendants Rasier, LLC, Rasier-Ca, LLC, Uber Technologies, Inc.'s Motion For Relief For Waiver Of Objections Pro Tem Judge Katherine Gallo, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Continued to August 19, 2020. Judge Pro Tem Gallo recuses herself from this case. As a result of the COVID-19 emergency, all attorneys and parties are required to appear remotely. Hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. To appear at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to Klgallo@discoveryreferee.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If parties have arranged for a court reporter to appear, please include the following in your email: (1) ensure that they are California Certified Shorthand Reporters; (2) the reporter's full contact information [name, CSR number, personal work email, and phone number]. If the reporter is not a California Certified Shorthand Reporter, the transcript cannot be certified for use in California courts. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must e-mail it to the Judge Pro Tem. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: MARIA A. CARVAJAL VS. SF MISSION PROPERTIES, LLC ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER to CROSS COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion Calendar for Friday, August 7, 2020, Line 16, CROSS-DEFENDANTS HUMBERTO NAVARRO AND WILLIAM JOHNSTON'S DEMURRER TO CROSS COMPLAINT. Humberto Navarro and William Johnston's "demurrer to cross-complaint" is ordered off calendar for failure to comply with CCP §430.41. Counsel's declaration says he "attempted to meet and confer" with opposing counsel by exchanging e-mails. But §430.41 requires lawyers to meet and confer "in person or by telephone." Counsel's declaration also says opposing counsel did not respond to his attempts at contact. Counsel for both sides are thus ordered to obey the law by immediately meeting and conferring as required. Humberto Navarro and William Johnston's response to the cross-complaint is now due on or before September 4, 2020. As a result of the COVID-19 emergency, all attorneys and parties are required to appear remotely. Hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. To appear at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. =(302/RBU)

rulings.Case Title: ANDREW M. JORDAN, INC. VS. LENDLEASE (US) CONSTRUCTION, INC.
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion Calendar for Friday, August 7, 2020, Line 10, DEFENDANTS LENDLEASE (US) CONSTRUCTION, INC., LIBERTY MUTUAL INSURANCE COMPANY, FIDELITY AND DEPOSIT COMPANY OF MARYLAND, AND AMERICAN HOME ASSURANCE COMPANY'S MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT. Defendants' "motion to strike plaintiff's complaint" is denied. The paragraph defendants seek to strike is essential to the breach of contract cause of action, so it would be error to strike it. (Quiroz v. Seventh Ave. Center (2006) 140 Cal.App.4th 1256, 1281.) Had defendants instead filed a demurrer, it would have been overruled, because demurrer challenges are limited to defects on a pleading's face. (Blank v. Kirwan (1985) 39 Cal.3d 311, 318.) Defendants represent that a contract was "incorporated by reference" into the complaint, but I do not find that language in the complaint, and the contract is not attached to the complaint. Nor may interpretation of a contract be accomplished by judicial notice. (Fremont Indemnity Co. v. Fremont General Corp. (2007) 148 Cal.App.4th 97, 113.) As a result of the COVID-19 emergency, all attorneys and parties are required to appear remotely. Hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. To appear at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the plaintiff is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/RBU)

rulings.Case Title: SKILLZ, INC. VS. KNOTEL, INC.
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For A Preliminary Injunction
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion Calendar for Friday, August 7, 2020, Line 13, PLAINTIFF SKILLZ, INC.'S Motion For A Preliminary Injunction. Plaintiff Skillz, Inc.'s motion for a preliminary injunction is denied. Defendant Knotel, Inc. developed a workspace for Skillz in San Francisco, which was to be substantially complete by May 31, 2020. Knotel asserts that the workspace was substantially complete by then, Skillz that it was not. Skillz would enjoin Knotel from "using, disbursing, transferring dissipating or otherwise encumbering or alienating" a refundable deposit that Skillz made on the project. Skillz has not carried its burden to show that it would prevail at trial. (Husain v. McDonald's Corp. (2012) 205 Cal.App.4th 860, 866-67.) Knotel adduces evidence that, during a May 21, 2020 walkthrough, Skillz expressed no concern about the workplace's state and instead complimented Knotel's ability to persevere during the Covid-19 pandemic. (Morningstar Dec. Paras. 14-15.) In turn, Skillz's motion relies solely on its complaint's allegations. Nor has Skillz shown that the balance of harms favors it. (Husain, 205 Cal.App.4th at 866-67.) Skillz argues that Knotel is financially troubled, relying solely on allegations "on information and belief." But such allegations cannot support an injunction. (Ancora-Citronelle Corp. v. Green (1974) 41 Cal.App.3d 146, 150 n.1.) Moreover, Knotel has provided a thorough declaration regarding its financial condition from its CFO. (Jureller Dec.) As a result of the COVID-19 emergency, all attorneys and parties are required to appear remotely. Hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. To appear at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the defendant is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/RBU)

rulings.Case Title: CHRISTIAN C.H. YUE VS. MIKE NGUYEN ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant Mike Nguyen Demurrer To Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion Calendar for Friday, August 7, 2020, Line 15, Defendant Mike Nguyen's Demurrer To Complaint. Defendant Mike Nguyen's demurrers are overruled in part and sustained in part: 1. The termination notice repeatedly makes clear that this is an owner move-in eviction; defendant cites no authority for his notion that the term "dominant motive" must be expressly pled. Demurrer overruled. 2. Plaintiff effectively concedes that he has not pled a landlord-tenant relationship with defendant. Demurrer sustained with leave to amend. 3. An annual rent increase "in and of itself" - without an addendum or attachment - changes lease terms. (Civ. Code §827.) Thus, no addendum or attachment would be expected here. Demurrer overruled. 4. On its face, San Francisco Rent Ordinance (SFRO) §37.9B(c)(4) merely requires a "description" of property and not the detail defendant advocates; no contrary authority is cited. Demurrer overruled. 5. In an owner move-in case, the SFRO's plain language and rent board regulations only require a declaration from "the" landlord who is moving in. (SFRO §37.9(a)(8)(v), RBR §12.14(b)(8).) Defendant cites no contrary authority. Demurrer overruled. As a result of the COVID-19 emergency, all attorneys and parties are required to appear remotely. Hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. To appear at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the plaintiff is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/RBU)
